gedcomtools
gedcomtools

Contents

  • Getting Started
  • GedcomX API
  • GEDCOM 5.x API
  • GEDCOM 7 API
  • CLI Reference
Back to top
View this page

GEDCOM 7 API¶

This module parses GEDCOM 7 files into an in-memory tree and exposes validation and serialization entry points.

Validation currently focuses on:

  • line parsing

  • hierarchy integrity

  • top-level file structure

  • legal child tags

  • child cardinality

  • pointer validation

  • selected enumeration validation

  • payload format validation

  • orphaned record detection

The docstrings are written in Google style so they render well with Sphinx Napoleon.

class gedcomtools.gedcom7.gedcom7.GedcomValidationError(code, message, line_num=None, tag=None, severity='error')[source]¶

Bases: object

Represents a validation problem.

code¶

Stable machine-readable code.

message¶

Human-readable description.

line_num¶

Source line number if available.

tag¶

GEDCOM tag associated with the problem.

severity¶

"error" or "warning".

code: str¶
message: str¶
line_num: int | None¶
tag: str | None¶
severity: str¶
class gedcomtools.gedcom7.gedcom7.Gedcom7(filepath=None)[source]¶

Bases: object

Parse and validate GEDCOM 7 files.

__init__(filepath=None)[source]¶

Initialize the parser.

Parameters:

filepath (Union[str, Path, None]) – Optional GEDCOM file path to load immediately.

filepath: Path | None¶
records: List[GedcomStructure]¶
errors: List[GedcomValidationError]¶
static parse_gedcom_line(line)[source]¶

Parse one GEDCOM line into normalized fields.

Parameters:

line (str) – Raw GEDCOM line.

Return type:

Optional[Dict[str, Any]]

Returns:

Parsed line dictionary or None for blank lines.

Raises:

ValueError – If the line is malformed.

loadfile(filepath)[source]¶

Load and parse a GEDCOM file.

Parameters:

filepath (Union[str, Path]) – Path to the GEDCOM file.

Raises:

GedcomParseError – If the file cannot be opened or read.

Return type:

None

parse_string(text)[source]¶

Parse GEDCOM 7 content from a string.

This resets any previously loaded data. Useful for testing or when GEDCOM content is available in memory rather than on disk.

Parameters:

text (str) – Complete GEDCOM 7 file content as a string.

Return type:

None

parse_lines(lines)[source]¶

Parse GEDCOM 7 content from an iterable of raw lines.

This resets any previously loaded data.

Parameters:

lines (Iterable[str]) – Iterable of raw GEDCOM lines (with or without newlines).

Return type:

None

validate()[source]¶

Validate the loaded GEDCOM tree.

Return type:

List[GedcomValidationError]

Returns:

All parse errors plus structural errors and warnings from the validator. Check issue.severity ("error" or "warning") to distinguish them.

If the file is not a GEDCOM 7 file a single error is returned instead of running the full validator (which would produce thousands of false positives on GEDCOM 5.x files).

detect_gedcom_version()[source]¶

Return the GEDCOM version declared in HEAD.GEDC.VERS.

Return type:

Optional[str]

Returns:

Version string if present, otherwise None.

write(filepath, *, line_ending='\\n', bom=False)[source]¶

Write the loaded records to a GEDCOM 7 file.

Parameters:

  • filepath (Union[str, Path]) – Destination path.

  • line_ending (str) – Line terminator (default LF per GEDCOM 7 spec).

  • bom (bool) – Whether to write a UTF-8 BOM (discouraged by the spec).

Return type:

None

individuals()[source]¶

Return every INDI record as a raw GedcomStructure.

Return type:

List[GedcomStructure]

get_individual(xref)[source]¶

Return the raw GedcomStructure for a single INDI by xref.

Return type:

Optional[GedcomStructure]

families()[source]¶

Return every FAM record as a raw GedcomStructure.

Return type:

List[GedcomStructure]

get_family(xref)[source]¶

Return the raw GedcomStructure for a single FAM by xref.

Return type:

Optional[GedcomStructure]

sources()[source]¶

Return every SOUR record as a raw GedcomStructure.

Return type:

List[GedcomStructure]

get_source(xref)[source]¶

Return the raw GedcomStructure for a single SOUR by xref.

Return type:

Optional[GedcomStructure]

repositories()[source]¶

Return every REPO record as a raw GedcomStructure.

Return type:

List[GedcomStructure]

get_repository(xref)[source]¶

Return the raw GedcomStructure for a single REPO by xref.

Return type:

Optional[GedcomStructure]

media_objects()[source]¶

Return every OBJE record as a raw GedcomStructure.

Return type:

List[GedcomStructure]

get_media(xref)[source]¶

Return the raw GedcomStructure for a single OBJE by xref.

Return type:

Optional[GedcomStructure]

shared_notes()[source]¶

Return every SNOTE record as a raw GedcomStructure.

Return type:

List[GedcomStructure]

get_shared_note(xref)[source]¶

Return the raw GedcomStructure for a single SNOTE by xref.

Return type:

Optional[GedcomStructure]

submitters()[source]¶

Return every SUBM record as a raw GedcomStructure.

Return type:

List[GedcomStructure]

get_submitter(xref)[source]¶

Return the raw GedcomStructure for a single SUBM by xref.

Return type:

Optional[GedcomStructure]

individual_details()[source]¶

Return IndividualDetail for every INDI record.

Return type:

List[IndividualDetail]

get_individual_detail(xref)[source]¶

Return IndividualDetail for a single INDI by xref.

Return type:

Optional[IndividualDetail]

family_details()[source]¶

Return FamilyDetail for every FAM record.

Return type:

List[FamilyDetail]

get_family_detail(xref)[source]¶

Return FamilyDetail for a single FAM by xref.

Return type:

Optional[FamilyDetail]

source_details()[source]¶

Return SourceDetail for every SOUR record.

Return type:

List[SourceDetail]

get_source_detail(xref)[source]¶

Return SourceDetail for a single SOUR by xref.

Return type:

Optional[SourceDetail]

repository_details()[source]¶

Return RepositoryDetail for every REPO record.

Return type:

List[RepositoryDetail]

get_repository_detail(xref)[source]¶

Return RepositoryDetail for a single REPO by xref.

Return type:

Optional[RepositoryDetail]

media_details()[source]¶

Return MediaDetail for every OBJE record.

Return type:

List[MediaDetail]

get_media_detail(xref)[source]¶

Return MediaDetail for a single OBJE by xref.

Return type:

Optional[MediaDetail]

shared_note_details()[source]¶

Return SharedNoteDetail for every SNOTE record.

Return type:

List[SharedNoteDetail]

get_shared_note_detail(xref)[source]¶

Return SharedNoteDetail for a single SNOTE by xref.

Return type:

Optional[SharedNoteDetail]

submitter_details()[source]¶

Return SubmitterDetail for every SUBM record.

Return type:

List[SubmitterDetail]

get_submitter_detail(xref)[source]¶

Return SubmitterDetail for a single SUBM by xref.

Return type:

Optional[SubmitterDetail]

get_parents(indi_xref)[source]¶

Return the parents of an individual as raw records.

Walks FAMC → FAM → HUSB/WIFE.

Parameters:

indi_xref (str) – Xref id of the individual (e.g. "@I1@").

Return type:

List[GedcomStructure]

Returns:

Raw GedcomStructure for each parent found.

get_children_of(indi_xref)[source]¶

Return the children of an individual as raw records.

Walks FAMS → FAM → CHIL.

Parameters:

indi_xref (str) – Xref id of the individual.

Return type:

List[GedcomStructure]

Returns:

Raw GedcomStructure for each child found.

get_spouses(indi_xref)[source]¶

Return the spouses of an individual as raw records.

For each FAMS family, returns the other HUSB or WIFE record.

Parameters:

indi_xref (str) – Xref id of the individual.

Return type:

List[GedcomStructure]

Returns:

Raw GedcomStructure for each spouse found.

get_parents_detail(indi_xref)[source]¶

Return parents as IndividualDetail snapshots.

Return type:

List[IndividualDetail]

get_children_detail(indi_xref)[source]¶

Return children as IndividualDetail snapshots.

Return type:

List[IndividualDetail]

get_spouses_detail(indi_xref)[source]¶

Return spouses as IndividualDetail snapshots.

Return type:

List[IndividualDetail]

to_dict()[source]¶

Convert the full GEDCOM file into a serializable dictionary.

Return type:

Dict[str, Any]

Returns:

Serializable representation of the parsed GEDCOM file.

This module defines the in-memory node representation used by the GEDCOM 7 parser and validator.

The design goals are:

  • preserve the original GEDCOM line semantics

  • keep xref ids separate from pointer payloads

  • make parent/child traversal easy

  • support validation against the GEDCOM 7 specification registry

The docstrings are written in Google style so they render well with Sphinx Napoleon.

class gedcomtools.gedcom7.structure.GedcomStructure(*, level, tag, xref_id=None, payload=None, payload_is_pointer=False, parent=None, line_num=None)[source]¶

Bases: object

Represents a single GEDCOM structure node.

level¶

GEDCOM nesting level for the structure.

tag¶

GEDCOM tag such as INDI or NAME.

xref_id¶

Optional xref id defined on the line, such as @I1@.

payload¶

Raw payload text following the tag.

payload_is_pointer¶

Whether the payload is a pointer such as @F1@.

parent¶

Parent structure, if any.

children¶

Child structures.

line_num¶

Original source line number.

uri¶

Resolved GEDCOM 7 URI where available.

extension¶

Whether the tag is an extension tag.

version = 'v7'¶
__init__(*, level, tag, xref_id=None, payload=None, payload_is_pointer=False, parent=None, line_num=None)[source]¶

Initialize the GEDCOM structure node.

Parameters:

  • level (int) – GEDCOM line level.

  • tag (str) – GEDCOM tag.

  • xref_id (Optional[str]) – Optional xref id defined on the line.

  • payload (Optional[str]) – Optional payload text.

  • payload_is_pointer (bool) – Whether the payload is a pointer.

  • parent (Optional[GedcomStructure]) – Parent structure.

  • line_num (Optional[int]) – Source line number.

property value: str¶

Return the line payload.

property pointer_target: str | None¶

Return the payload if it is a pointer.

add_child(child)[source]¶

Attach a child node.

Parameters:

child (GedcomStructure) – Child structure to attach.

Return type:

None

get_children(tag)[source]¶

Return direct children for a tag.

Parameters:

tag (str) – GEDCOM tag.

Return type:

List[GedcomStructure]

Returns:

Matching child structures.

first_child(tag)[source]¶

Return the first direct child matching a tag.

Parameters:

tag (str) – GEDCOM tag.

Return type:

Optional[GedcomStructure]

Returns:

First matching child or None.

property depth: int¶

Return the nesting depth (0 for top-level records).

Returns:

Number of ancestor nodes above this node.

get_path()[source]¶

Return a human-readable path from the root to this node.

Return type:

str

Returns:

Slash-separated path of tags and xref ids.

get_ancestor(tag)[source]¶

Walk up the tree and return the first ancestor with the given tag.

Parameters:

tag (str) – GEDCOM tag to search for.

Return type:

Optional[GedcomStructure]

Returns:

Matching ancestor node or None.

to_dict()[source]¶

Convert the node and descendants to a serializable dictionary.

Return type:

Dict[str, Any]

Returns:

Nested dictionary representation of the structure tree.

High-level view objects for GEDCOM 7 records.

These are read-only snapshots — they are built from a GedcomStructure tree at construction time and do not stay in sync if the tree is later mutated.

Usage:

from gedcomtools.gedcom7 import Gedcom7
from gedcomtools.gedcom7.models import individual_detail, family_detail

g = Gedcom7("family.ged")

# by xref
person = g.get_individual("@I1@")
print(person.full_name, person.birth_year, person.death_year)

# iterate all
for p in g.individuals():
    print(p.full_name, p.sex)

for f in g.families():
    print(f.xref, f.husband_xref, f.wife_xref, len(f.children_xrefs))
class gedcomtools.gedcom7.models.EventDetail(date=None, place=None, age=None, cause=None, event_type=None, agency=None, note=None, sources=<factory>, shared_note_refs=<factory>, media_refs=<factory>)[source]

Bases: object

A genealogical event (birth, death, marriage, etc.).

date

Raw GEDCOM date string, e.g. "1 JAN 2000" or "ABT 1850".

place

Place name from the PLAC substructure.

age

Age of the individual at the event (AGE substructure).

cause

Cause of the event (CAUS — typically used for death).

event_type

Free-text TYPE qualifier attached to the event node.

agency

AGNC substructure value.

note

First inline NOTE payload, if any.

date: str | None = None
place: str | None = None
age: str | None = None
cause: str | None = None
event_type: str | None = None
agency: str | None = None
note: str | None = None
sources: List[SourceCitation]
shared_note_refs: List[str]
media_refs: List[str]
property year: int | None

Extract the four-digit year from the date string, or None.

property qualifier: str | None

ABT, BEF, AFT, CAL, EST, etc.

Type:

Return any date qualifier prefix

property age_years: int | None

Extract the year component from the AGE string (e.g. "45y 3m" → 45).

class gedcomtools.gedcom7.models.NameDetail(full='', given=None, surname=None, prefix=None, suffix=None, nickname=None, surname_prefix=None, name_type=None)[source]

Bases: object

A single name entry from an INDI NAME structure.

full

Raw GEDCOM NAME payload, e.g. "Lt. /de Allen/ jr.".

given

GIVN substructure value.

surname

SURN substructure value.

prefix

NPFX name prefix (title before name).

suffix

NSFX name suffix (Jr., III, etc.).

nickname

NICK substructure value.

surname_prefix

SPFX surname prefix (de, van, von, etc.).

name_type

TYPE substructure value (BIRTH, AKA, IMMIGRANT, etc.).

full: str = ''
given: str | None = None
surname: str | None = None
prefix: str | None = None
suffix: str | None = None
nickname: str | None = None
surname_prefix: str | None = None
name_type: str | None = None
property display: str

Clean display name with GEDCOM surname slashes removed.

class gedcomtools.gedcom7.models.SourceCitation(xref, page=None, quality=None)[source]

Bases: object

A source citation attached to a record or event.

xref

Pointer to the SOUR record (e.g. "@S1@").

page

PAGE substructure value (where within the source).

quality

QUAY substructure value (0–3 quality assessment).

xref: str
page: str | None = None
quality: str | None = None
class gedcomtools.gedcom7.models.IndividualDetail(xref, names=<factory>, sex=None, birth=None, christening=None, death=None, burial=None, cremation=None, occupation=None, title=None, religion=None, nationality=None, residences=<factory>, events=<factory>, families_as_child=<factory>, families_as_spouse=<factory>, source_citations=<factory>, note_texts=<factory>, shared_note_refs=<factory>, media_refs=<factory>, uid=None, restriction=None, last_changed=None, _record=None, _save_fn=None)[source]

Bases: object

Aggregated view of a GEDCOM 7 INDI record.

All repeated structures (names, residences, events, source citations) are collected into lists. Single-valued fields like birth and death use the first occurrence found.

xref

Xref id, e.g. "@I1@".

names

All NAME entries, primary first.

sex

SEX payload ("M", "F", "X", "U").

birth

First BIRT event.

christening

First CHR event.

death

First DEAT event.

burial

First BURI event.

cremation

First CREM event.

occupation

First OCCU payload.

title

First TITL payload.

religion

First RELI payload.

nationality

First NATI payload.

residences

All RESI events.

events

All generic EVEN structures.

families_as_child

FAMC pointer values.

families_as_spouse

FAMS pointer values.

source_citations

All SOUR citations on the INDI record itself.

note_texts

Inline NOTE payloads (not shared-note pointers).

shared_note_refs

SNOTE pointer values.

media_refs

OBJE pointer values.

uid

First UID payload.

restriction

RESN payload.

last_changed

CHAN.DATE payload.

xref: str
names: List[NameDetail]
sex: str | None = None
birth: EventDetail | None = None
christening: EventDetail | None = None
death: EventDetail | None = None
burial: EventDetail | None = None
cremation: EventDetail | None = None
occupation: str | None = None
title: str | None = None
religion: str | None = None
nationality: str | None = None
residences: List[EventDetail]
events: List[EventDetail]
families_as_child: List[str]
families_as_spouse: List[str]
source_citations: List[SourceCitation]
note_texts: List[str]
shared_note_refs: List[str]
media_refs: List[str]
uid: str | None = None
restriction: str | None = None
last_changed: str | None = None
save()[source]

Write Detail fields back to the underlying raw record.

Raises:

RuntimeError – If this Detail was not obtained via a parser accessor (i.e. has no attached record).

Return type:

None

property name: NameDetail | None

Primary (first) name entry.

property full_name: str

Clean display name of the primary name entry.

property birth_year: int | None

Year extracted from the birth date, or None.

property death_year: int | None

Year extracted from the death date, or None.

property is_living: bool

True when no death event is recorded.

property age_at_death: int | None

Approximate age at death computed from birth/death years.

class gedcomtools.gedcom7.models.FamilyDetail(xref, husband_xref=None, wife_xref=None, children_xrefs=<factory>, marriage=None, divorce=None, events=<factory>, source_citations=<factory>, note_texts=<factory>, shared_note_refs=<factory>, media_refs=<factory>, uid=None, restriction=None, last_changed=None, _record=None, _save_fn=None)[source]

Bases: object

Aggregated view of a GEDCOM 7 FAM record.

xref

Xref id, e.g. "@F1@".

husband_xref

HUSB pointer value.

wife_xref

WIFE pointer value.

children_xrefs

CHIL pointer values (in document order).

marriage

First MARR event.

divorce

First DIV event.

events

All generic EVEN structures.

source_citations

SOUR citations on the FAM record.

note_texts

Inline NOTE payloads.

shared_note_refs

SNOTE pointer values.

media_refs

OBJE pointer values.

uid

First UID payload.

restriction

RESN payload.

last_changed

CHAN.DATE payload.

xref: str
husband_xref: str | None = None
wife_xref: str | None = None
children_xrefs: List[str]
marriage: EventDetail | None = None
divorce: EventDetail | None = None
events: List[EventDetail]
source_citations: List[SourceCitation]
note_texts: List[str]
shared_note_refs: List[str]
media_refs: List[str]
uid: str | None = None
restriction: str | None = None
last_changed: str | None = None
save()[source]

Write Detail fields back to the underlying raw record.

Return type:

None

property marriage_year: int | None

Year extracted from the marriage date, or None.

property divorce_year: int | None

Year extracted from the divorce date, or None.

property num_children: int

Number of children listed in the family record.

class gedcomtools.gedcom7.models.SourceDetail(xref, title=None, author=None, publication=None, abbreviation=None, repository_refs=<factory>, note_texts=<factory>, shared_note_refs=<factory>, media_refs=<factory>, uid=None, last_changed=None, _record=None, _save_fn=None)[source]

Bases: object

Aggregated view of a GEDCOM 7 SOUR record.

xref

Xref id.

title

TITL payload.

author

AUTH payload.

publication

PUBL payload.

abbreviation

ABBR payload.

repository_refs

REPO pointer values.

note_texts

Inline NOTE payloads.

shared_note_refs

SNOTE pointer values.

media_refs

OBJE pointer values.

uid

First UID payload.

last_changed

CHAN.DATE payload.

xref: str
title: str | None = None
author: str | None = None
publication: str | None = None
abbreviation: str | None = None
repository_refs: List[str]
note_texts: List[str]
shared_note_refs: List[str]
media_refs: List[str]
uid: str | None = None
last_changed: str | None = None
save()[source]

Write Detail fields back to the underlying raw record.

Return type:

None

class gedcomtools.gedcom7.models.RepositoryDetail(xref, name=None, address=None, phone=None, email=None, website=None, note_texts=<factory>, shared_note_refs=<factory>, uid=None, last_changed=None, _record=None, _save_fn=None)[source]

Bases: object

Aggregated view of a GEDCOM 7 REPO record.

xref

Xref id.

name

NAME payload.

address

ADDR payload.

phone

First PHON payload.

email

First EMAIL payload.

website

First WWW payload.

note_texts

Inline NOTE payloads.

shared_note_refs

SNOTE pointer values.

uid

First UID payload.

last_changed

CHAN.DATE payload.

xref: str
name: str | None = None
address: str | None = None
phone: str | None = None
email: str | None = None
website: str | None = None
note_texts: List[str]
shared_note_refs: List[str]
uid: str | None = None
last_changed: str | None = None
save()[source]

Write Detail fields back to the underlying raw record.

Return type:

None

class gedcomtools.gedcom7.models.MediaDetail(xref, files=<factory>, title=None, note_texts=<factory>, shared_note_refs=<factory>, uid=None, last_changed=None, _record=None, _save_fn=None)[source]

Bases: object

Aggregated view of a GEDCOM 7 OBJE record.

xref

Xref id.

files

List of (file_path, media_type) tuples from FILE substructures.

title

TITL payload.

note_texts

Inline NOTE payloads.

shared_note_refs

SNOTE pointer values.

uid

First UID payload.

last_changed

CHAN.DATE payload.

xref: str
files: List[Tuple[str, str | None]]
title: str | None = None
note_texts: List[str]
shared_note_refs: List[str]
uid: str | None = None
last_changed: str | None = None
save()[source]

Write Detail fields back to the underlying raw record.

Return type:

None

class gedcomtools.gedcom7.models.SharedNoteDetail(xref, text='', mime=None, language=None, source_citations=<factory>, uid=None, last_changed=None, _record=None, _save_fn=None)[source]

Bases: object

Aggregated view of a GEDCOM 7 SNOTE record.

xref

Xref id.

text

Full note text (CONT lines already merged).

mime

MIME type if declared.

language

LANG payload.

source_citations

SOUR citations.

uid

First UID payload.

last_changed

CHAN.DATE payload.

xref: str
text: str = ''
mime: str | None = None
language: str | None = None
source_citations: List[SourceCitation]
uid: str | None = None
last_changed: str | None = None
save()[source]

Write Detail fields back to the underlying raw record.

Return type:

None

class gedcomtools.gedcom7.models.SubmitterDetail(xref, name=None, address=None, phone=None, email=None, website=None, language=None, note_texts=<factory>, shared_note_refs=<factory>, uid=None, last_changed=None, _record=None, _save_fn=None)[source]

Bases: object

Aggregated view of a GEDCOM 7 SUBM record.

xref

Xref id.

name

NAME payload.

address

ADDR payload.

phone

First PHON payload.

email

First EMAIL payload.

website

First WWW payload.

language

First LANG payload.

note_texts

Inline NOTE payloads.

shared_note_refs

SNOTE pointer values.

uid

First UID payload.

last_changed

CHAN.DATE payload.

xref: str
name: str | None = None
address: str | None = None
phone: str | None = None
email: str | None = None
website: str | None = None
language: str | None = None
note_texts: List[str]
shared_note_refs: List[str]
uid: str | None = None
last_changed: str | None = None
save()[source]

Write Detail fields back to the underlying raw record.

Return type:

None

gedcomtools.gedcom7.models.individual_detail(node)[source]

Build an IndividualDetail from an INDI GedcomStructure.

Parameters:

node (GedcomStructure) – A top-level INDI structure.

Return type:

IndividualDetail

Returns:

Populated IndividualDetail snapshot.

gedcomtools.gedcom7.models.family_detail(node)[source]

Build a FamilyDetail from a FAM GedcomStructure.

Parameters:

node (GedcomStructure) – A top-level FAM structure.

Return type:

FamilyDetail

Returns:

Populated FamilyDetail snapshot.

gedcomtools.gedcom7.models.source_detail(node)[source]

Build a SourceDetail from a SOUR GedcomStructure.

Parameters:

node (GedcomStructure) – A top-level SOUR structure.

Return type:

SourceDetail

Returns:

Populated SourceDetail snapshot.

gedcomtools.gedcom7.models.repository_detail(node)[source]

Build a RepositoryDetail from a REPO GedcomStructure.

Parameters:

node (GedcomStructure) – A top-level REPO structure.

Return type:

RepositoryDetail

Returns:

Populated RepositoryDetail snapshot.

gedcomtools.gedcom7.models.media_detail(node)[source]

Build a MediaDetail from an OBJE GedcomStructure.

Parameters:

node (GedcomStructure) – A top-level OBJE structure.

Return type:

MediaDetail

Returns:

Populated MediaDetail snapshot.

gedcomtools.gedcom7.models.shared_note_detail(node)[source]

Build a SharedNoteDetail from an SNOTE GedcomStructure.

Parameters:

node (GedcomStructure) – A top-level SNOTE structure.

Return type:

SharedNoteDetail

Returns:

Populated SharedNoteDetail snapshot.

gedcomtools.gedcom7.models.submitter_detail(node)[source]

Build a SubmitterDetail from a SUBM GedcomStructure.

Parameters:

node (GedcomStructure) – A top-level SUBM structure.

Return type:

SubmitterDetail

Returns:

Populated SubmitterDetail snapshot.

This module provides a normalized rule layer that sits on top of the raw GEDCOM 7 tag/URI mapping. It intentionally starts with a practical core rule set that is enough to parse and validate common GEDCOM 7 files while still being easy to extend.

The structure registry in this file is used by the parser and validator for:

  • top-level legality checks

  • parent/child legality checks

  • child cardinality checks

  • basic payload typing

  • selected enumeration checks

The docstrings are written in Google style so they render well with Sphinx Napoleon.

gedcomtools.gedcom7.specification.get_spec(key)[source]¶

Return a normalized specification entry.

Parameters:

key (Optional[str]) – GEDCOM tag or URI.

Return type:

Dict[str, Any]

Returns:

Normalized specification mapping or an empty dict.

gedcomtools.gedcom7.specification.get_tag(key)[source]¶

Resolve a tag from either a tag or a URI.

Parameters:

key (str) – GEDCOM tag or URI.

Return type:

str

Returns:

Resolved GEDCOM tag.

gedcomtools.gedcom7.specification.get_uri(key)[source]¶

Resolve a URI from either a tag or a URI.

Parameters:

key (str) – GEDCOM tag or URI.

Return type:

Optional[str]

Returns:

URI or None if unknown.

gedcomtools.gedcom7.specification.get_label(key)[source]¶

Return a human-readable display label for a GEDCOM tag or URI.

Parameters:

key (Optional[str]) – GEDCOM tag (e.g. "BIRT") or URI.

Return type:

str

Returns:

Human-readable label such as "Birth", or the tag itself if no label is registered.

gedcomtools.gedcom7.specification.top_level_tags()[source]¶

Return the known top-level record tags.

Return type:

List[str]

gedcomtools.gedcom7.specification.allowed_child_map(parent_key)[source]¶

Return the allowed child map for a parent.

Parameters:

parent_key (Optional[str]) – Parent tag or URI.

Return type:

Dict[str, str]

Returns:

Mapping of child tag to child identifier.

gedcomtools.gedcom7.specification.allowed_child_tags(parent_key)[source]¶

Return the allowed child tags for a parent.

Parameters:

parent_key (Optional[str]) – Parent tag or URI.

Return type:

List[str]

Returns:

Sorted child tags.

gedcomtools.gedcom7.specification.is_allowed_child(parent_key, child_tag)[source]¶

Return whether a child tag is legal under a parent.

Parameters:

  • parent_key (Optional[str]) – Parent tag or URI.

  • child_tag (str) – Candidate child tag.

Return type:

bool

Returns:

True if the child is legal, else False.

gedcomtools.gedcom7.specification.get_cardinality(parent_key, child_tag)[source]¶

Return cardinality for a child under a parent.

Parameters:

  • parent_key (Optional[str]) – Parent tag or URI.

  • child_tag (str) – Child GEDCOM tag.

Return type:

Optional[tuple[int, Optional[int]]]

Returns:

(min_count, max_count) or None if unspecified.

gedcomtools.gedcom7.specification.get_payload_type(key)[source]¶

Return the normalized payload type for a structure.

Parameters:

key (Optional[str]) – GEDCOM tag or URI.

Return type:

Optional[str]

Returns:

Payload type or None.

gedcomtools.gedcom7.specification.get_enum_values(key)[source]¶

Return valid enumeration values for a structure.

Parameters:

key (Optional[str]) – GEDCOM tag or URI.

Return type:

Optional[set[str]]

Returns:

Set of valid values or None.

gedcomtools.gedcom7.specification.get_context_enum_values(tag, parent_tag)[source]¶

Return context-specific enumeration values when the same tag has different allowed values depending on its parent structure.

Parameters:

  • tag (str) – GEDCOM tag.

  • parent_tag (Optional[str]) – Tag of the parent structure, or None for top-level.

Return type:

Optional[frozenset]

Returns:

Frozenset of valid values if a context-specific rule exists, otherwise None (meaning fall through to the general enum check).

gedcomtools.gedcom7.specification.register_extension_tag(tag, uri)[source]¶

Register an extension tag from HEAD.SCHMA.TAG.

Parameters:

  • tag (str) – Extension tag such as _FOO.

  • uri (str) – Corresponding extension URI.

Return type:

None

This module performs structural and semantic validation of parsed GEDCOM 7 trees.

Validation phases:

  1. file-level validation

  2. parent/child legality validation

  3. child cardinality validation

  4. basic payload validation

  5. pointer validation

  6. selected enumeration validation

  7. payload format validation (DATE, TIME, AGE, LATI, LONG, LANG, RESN)

  8. orphaned record detection

  9. bidirectional pointer consistency

  10. TRAN context validation

The docstrings are written in Google style so they render well with Sphinx Napoleon.

class gedcomtools.gedcom7.validator.ValidationIssue(code, message, line_num=None, tag=None, severity='error')[source]

Bases: object

Represents a GEDCOM validation issue.

code

Stable machine-readable issue code.

message

Human-readable issue description.

line_num

Source line number if available.

tag

GEDCOM tag associated with the issue.

severity

Issue severity. Usually "error" or "warning".

code: str
message: str
line_num: int | None
tag: str | None
severity: str
class gedcomtools.gedcom7.validator.GedcomValidator(records, *, strict_extensions=True)[source]

Bases: object

Validate a parsed GEDCOM 7 dataset.

Parameters:

  • records (List[GedcomStructure]) – Parsed top-level GEDCOM records.

  • strict_extensions (bool) – Whether undeclared extension tags should be errors.

__init__(records, *, strict_extensions=True)[source]

Initialize the validator.

Parameters:

  • records (List[GedcomStructure]) – Parsed top-level records.

  • strict_extensions (bool) – Whether extension tags require declaration.

issues: List[ValidationIssue]
add_issue(code, message, *, line_num=None, tag=None, severity='error')[source]

Add a validation issue.

Parameters:

  • code (str) – Stable machine-readable issue code.

  • message (str) – Human-readable issue text.

  • line_num (Optional[int]) – Source line number if available.

  • tag (Optional[str]) – GEDCOM tag associated with the issue.

  • severity (str) – Issue severity.

Return type:

None

validate()[source]

Run all validation phases.

Return type:

List[ValidationIssue]

Returns:

Collected validation issues.

walk()[source]

Yield all nodes in the dataset depth-first.

Return type:

Iterable[GedcomStructure]

walk_node(node)[source]

Yield a node and all descendants.

Parameters:

node (GedcomStructure) – Root node to walk.

Return type:

Iterable[GedcomStructure]

validate_file_structure()[source]

Validate required GEDCOM file-level rules.

Return type:

None

index_xrefs()[source]

Build an index of all defined xref ids.

Return type:

None

collect_declared_extension_tags()[source]

Collect extension tags declared in HEAD.SCHMA.TAG.

Return type:

None

validate_node(node)[source]

Validate a node and its descendants.

Parameters:

node (GedcomStructure) – Node to validate.

Return type:

None

validate_known_tag(node)[source]

Validate that a tag is known or an extension.

Parameters:

node (GedcomStructure) – Node to validate.

Return type:

None

validate_extension_usage(node)[source]

Validate extension tag declaration.

Parameters:

node (GedcomStructure) – Node to validate.

Return type:

None

validate_level(node)[source]

Validate level stepping.

Parameters:

node (GedcomStructure) – Node to validate.

Return type:

None

validate_child_legality(node)[source]

Validate parent/child legality.

Parameters:

node (GedcomStructure) – Node to validate.

Return type:

None

validate_cardinality(node)[source]

Validate child cardinality for a parent node.

Parameters:

node (GedcomStructure) – Parent node whose children should be checked.

Return type:

None

validate_payload(node)[source]

Validate basic payload typing.

Parameters:

node (GedcomStructure) – Node to validate.

Return type:

None

validate_enumeration(node)[source]

Validate selected enumeration payloads.

Checks the general enum for the tag first; if none is registered, falls back to context-specific enums keyed on (tag, parent_tag).

Parameters:

node (GedcomStructure) – Node to validate.

Return type:

None

validate_pointers()[source]

Validate pointer targets.

Return type:

None

validate_bidirectional_links()[source]

Validate bidirectional pointer consistency between INDI and FAM records.

Checks: - INDI.FAMC @FAM@ → that FAM must have CHIL @INDI@ pointing back - INDI.FAMS @FAM@ → that FAM must have HUSB or WIFE @INDI@ pointing back - FAM.CHIL @INDI@ → that INDI must have FAMC @FAM@ pointing back

Return type:

None

validate_tran_context(node)[source]

Validate TRAN substructures match their parent context.

TRAN under NOTE/SNOTE must have LANG, may have MIME. TRAN under NAME must have LANG, may have NAME-part subs (GIVN, SURN…). TRAN under PLAC must have LANG, may have PLAC subs (MAP, FORM…). TRAN under FILE must have LANG plus FORM and MIME.

Parameters:

node (GedcomStructure) – Node to validate (any node; only acts on TRAN nodes).

Return type:

None

validate_orphaned_records()[source]

Warn about top-level records that are defined but never cited.

Checks SOUR, REPO, OBJE, SNOTE records to see whether any pointer in the entire tree references their xref id.

Note: SUBM records referenced by HEAD.SUBM are treated as cited.

Return type:

None

validate_void_pointer(node)[source]

Warn if @VOID@ is used in a context where it is not meaningful.

@VOID@ is only appropriate for pointer-type payloads on tags that the GEDCOM 7 spec explicitly allows to point to @VOID@: FAMC, FAMS, CHIL, HUSB, WIFE, ASSO, SOUR (citation), OBJE (citation), REPO (citation), SUBM.

Parameters:

node (GedcomStructure) – Node to validate.

Return type:

None

validate_xref_format(node)[source]

Validate that a node’s xref id matches the required format.

Parameters:

node (GedcomStructure) – Node whose xref id should be validated.

Return type:

None

validate_payload_format(node)[source]

Validate format-specific payload constraints for a node.

Checks DATE, TIME, AGE, LATI, LONG, LANG, and RESN payloads.

Parameters:

node (GedcomStructure) – Node to validate.

Return type:

None

Serializes in-memory GedcomStructure trees back to valid GEDCOM 7 text.

Design notes¶

  • The parser merges CONT lines into the parent payload using \n as a separator. The writer splits those embedded newlines back into CONT substructures at level + 1.

  • CONC is not emitted; it was removed in GEDCOM 7.0. Very long single-line payloads are written as-is with a warning returned to the caller.

  • The class is intentionally stateless between calls so that a single writer instance can be re-used for many files or round-trip conversions.

  • Future converters (GEDCOM 5 → 7, GEDCOMx → 7) build a List[GedcomStructure] and pass it straight to this writer.

The docstrings are written in Google style so they render well with Sphinx Napoleon.

class gedcomtools.gedcom7.writer.Gedcom7Writer(*, line_ending='\\n', bom=False)[source]¶

Bases: object

Serialize GedcomStructure trees to GEDCOM 7 text.

Parameters:

  • line_ending (str) – Line terminator written after every GEDCOM line. GEDCOM 7 specifies U+000A (LF, "\n"). Use "\r\n" only when the target system requires CRLF.

  • bom (bool) – Whether to prepend a UTF-8 BOM (U+FEFF). The GEDCOM 7 specification discourages the BOM; the default is False.

Example:

from gedcomtools.gedcom7 import Gedcom7
from gedcomtools.gedcom7.writer import Gedcom7Writer

g = Gedcom7("family.ged")
writer = Gedcom7Writer()
writer.write(g.records, "output.ged")
text = writer.serialize(g.records)
get_warnings()[source]¶

Return line-length warnings from the last serialize() or write() call.

Return type:

List[str]

Returns:

List of human-readable warning strings.

write(records, filepath, *, encoding='utf-8')[source]¶

Write records to a file.

Parameters:

  • records (List[GedcomStructure]) – Top-level GEDCOM structures (typically HEAD … TRLR).

  • filepath (Union[str, Path]) – Destination file path. Any intermediate directories must already exist.

  • encoding (str) – File encoding. GEDCOM 7 mandates UTF-8; only change this for special debugging purposes.

Return type:

None

serialize(records)[source]¶

Serialize records to a GEDCOM 7 string.

Parameters:

records (List[GedcomStructure]) – Top-level GEDCOM structures.

Return type:

str

Returns:

The complete GEDCOM 7 file content as a str, including the final line terminator on the TRLR line.

This module provides normalized GEDCOM 7 tag and URI lookup helpers.

The core mapping is a flat G7_TAG_TO_URI dict. This module adds:

  • normalized lookup helpers

  • reverse lookup (URI → tag)

  • dynamic extension tag registration

  • convenience exports for the GEDCOM 7 parser and validator

The docstrings are written in Google style so they render well with Sphinx Napoleon.

gedcomtools.gedcom7.g7interop.normalize_tag(tag)[source]¶

Normalize a GEDCOM tag.

Parameters:

tag (str) – Raw GEDCOM tag value.

Return type:

str

Returns:

Uppercase, stripped GEDCOM tag.

Raises:

TypeError – If tag is not a string.

gedcomtools.gedcom7.g7interop.normalize_uri(uri)[source]¶

Normalize a GEDCOM URI.

Parameters:

uri (str) – Raw GEDCOM URI.

Return type:

str

Returns:

Stripped GEDCOM URI.

Raises:

TypeError – If uri is not a string.

gedcomtools.gedcom7.g7interop.get_uri_for_tag(tag)[source]¶

Return the canonical GEDCOM 7 URI for a tag.

Parameters:

tag (str) – GEDCOM tag.

Return type:

Optional[str]

Returns:

Matching URI or None if unknown.

gedcomtools.gedcom7.g7interop.get_tag_for_uri(uri)[source]¶

Return the canonical GEDCOM tag for a URI.

Parameters:

uri (str) – GEDCOM URI.

Return type:

Optional[str]

Returns:

Matching tag or None if unknown.

gedcomtools.gedcom7.g7interop.is_known_tag(tag)[source]¶

Return whether a GEDCOM tag is known.

Parameters:

tag (str) – GEDCOM tag.

Return type:

bool

Returns:

True if known, else False.

gedcomtools.gedcom7.g7interop.is_known_uri(uri)[source]¶

Return whether a GEDCOM URI is known.

Parameters:

uri (str) – GEDCOM URI.

Return type:

bool

Returns:

True if known, else False.

gedcomtools.gedcom7.g7interop.register_tag_uri(tag, uri, *, overwrite=False)[source]¶

Register a GEDCOM tag/URI mapping.

This is mainly used for extension tags declared in HEAD.SCHMA.TAG.

Parameters:

  • tag (str) – GEDCOM tag such as _FOO.

  • uri (str) – GEDCOM URI for the extension tag.

  • overwrite (bool) – Whether to overwrite an existing mapping.

Raises:

ValueError – If a conflicting mapping exists and overwrite is false.

Return type:

None

Exception classes for gedcom7.

exception gedcomtools.gedcom7.exceptions.GedcomError[source]¶

Bases: Exception

Base class for all GEDCOM 7 errors.

exception gedcomtools.gedcom7.exceptions.GedcomParseError[source]¶

Bases: GedcomError

Raised when a GEDCOM file cannot be opened or is structurally invalid.
